The baseline score is moderately positive, indicating some support for alliance commitment, but the attack presents a compelling argument that nudges the opinion slightly downward, questioning the fairness of current arrangements.

request_payload.exposure_text: 🚨 Our allies are taking advantage of us. While we maintain our end of the bargain, they're cutting defense budgets and leaving us to bear the burden. Just look at the numbers: NATO allies' defense spending has stagnated at just 1.5% of GDP, far below the 2% target. Meanwhile, our troops are stretched thin. We can't afford to be the world's policeman while others freeload. It's time to put America first and demand fair burden-sharing. Before another crisis hits, we need to strengthen our defenses and make sure our allies step up. #NationalSecurity #AllianceCommitment
